nodes.label: Whole Foods Restock Cadence

nodes.creator: people-ops-mgr

nodes.label: Mango Season Demand Spike
nodes.description: CEO directive: During May-August, stockpile Mango Puree instead of switching suppliers. Demand spikes 3x.
nodes.creator: people-ceo

nodes.label: CEO

nodes.description: Department level. Manages operations, store logistics, and inventory workflows.

nodes.label: Procurement Lead
nodes.description: Individual level. Handles supplier relationships, sourcing, and cost negotiations.

nodes.label: Leadership
nodes.description: Executive team. Sets strategic direction and resolves cross-department conflicts.

nodes.label: Operations
nodes.description: Manages supply chain, logistics, procurement, and store operations.

questions.question: TrueHarvest Co. shipment is 3 days late. What happens?
questions.answer: TrueHarvest is 3 days late, affecting 3 ingredients and 4 products. The Backup Supplier Playbook recommends switching to FreshFarm Co., but the CEO's Mango Season skill overrides this for Mango Puree during peak season. The system applies the switch for Apple Puree and Fresh Tomatoes only, preserving Mango Puree supply continuity per executive directive. Both the Ops Manager and Procurement Lead receive the decision with full reasoning.
reasoningSteps: TrueHarvest Co. flagged as 3 days late at supplier node.
Graph traversal: TrueHarvest supplies Mango Puree, Apple Puree, Fresh Tomatoes.
Products affected: Mango Chutney, Apple Cider Vinegar, Tomato Ketchup, Pesto Basilico.
Process node "Supplier Switch Rule" triggers on the late-shipment event.
Skill fires: "Backup Supplier Playbook" (Record Ingestion, Individual priority, created by Procurement Lead) recommends switching to FreshFarm Co. based on 47 past tickets.
Skill fires: "Mango Season Demand Spike" (Active Teaching, Executive priority, created by CEO) says to stockpile Mango Puree during peak season, not switch suppliers.
Conflict detected: two skills on the Mango Puree path disagree. System checks org hierarchy via Department nodes.

Resolution: CEO skill (Executive priority) overrides Procurement Lead skill (Individual priority). Stockpile recommendation wins for Mango Puree. FreshFarm Co. switch proceeds for Apple Puree and Fresh Tomatoes only.
People routed: Ops Manager and Procurement Lead both notified with resolution and full reasoning chain.

questions.question: East Coast Production Facility goes offline. What is affected?
questions.answer: The East Coast facility produces 4 products serving all major channels and 4 store accounts. Hot Sauce and Tomato Ketchup can reroute to Midwest Plant. Mango Chutney and Garlic Aioli face supply interruption - no alternative plant exists. The Wastage Protocol triggers perishable inventory checks. The Ops Manager's Thursday Liquidation skill recommends immediate markdowns at nearby stores to recover revenue from at-risk inventory. Whole Foods requires 48-hour advance notice per the Restock Cadence skill - notification deadline is flagged.
reasoningSteps: East Coast Production Facility marked offline.
Entity traversal: produces Mango Chutney, Tomato Ketchup, Hot Sauce 150ml, Garlic Aioli.
Channels affected: Retail Stores, E-Commerce DTC, Wholesale/B2B.
Stores affected: Whole Foods, Target, Mango Saree, White Vineyard.
Process node "Wastage Protocol" triggers - checks perishable inventory at the facility.

Skill fires: "Thursday Liquidation Timing" (Passive Observation, Department priority, created by Ops Manager) - markdowns on Thursdays at nearby retail stores recover 23% more revenue than weekend markdowns.

Skill fires: "Whole Foods Restock Cadence" (Conversational Learning, Department priority, created by Ops Manager) - Whole Foods requires 48-hour advance notice for schedule changes; system flags the notification deadline.
Midwest Processing Plant can absorb Hot Sauce and Tomato Ketchup. Mango Chutney and Garlic Aioli have NO alternative plant - production halts.
People routed: Ops Manager receives action plan; Procurement Lead receives supplier notification checklist.

questions.question: The Ops Manager just resigned. What knowledge is at risk?
questions.answer: The Ops Manager created 2 skills and was responsible for 2 production facilities. Both skills (Thursday Liquidation Timing and Whole Foods Restock Cadence) remain anchored to the graph and will continue to fire - no knowledge is lost. However, both skills carry Department-level priority, and with the Ops Manager's departure, Operations has no Department-level decision-maker. The system generates a handover report with full skill procedures, invocation history, and entity responsibilities for the incoming hire. The CEO is flagged about the org hierarchy gap.
reasoningSteps: Ops Manager People node flagged as departing.

System traces all skills created by Ops Manager: "Thursday Liquidation Timing" (Passive Observation, Department priority) anchored to Retail Stores + Liquidation Workflow. "Whole Foods Restock Cadence" (Conversational Learning, Department priority) anchored to Whole Foods + Restock Trigger.
System traces all entities the Ops Manager is RESPONSIBLE_FOR: East Coast Production Facility, Midwest Processing Plant.
Department node "Operations" checked - Procurement Lead also belongs to Operations but is Individual-level, not Department-level. No one inherits Department priority automatically.
Skills remain anchored to graph - they will continue to fire. But priority level may need reassignment.

System generates handover report: 2 skills created by Ops Manager (with full procedure, trigger conditions, invocation history), 2 facilities under their responsibility. Recommendation: incoming Operations lead should review and confirm both skills within 30 days.
CEO (via Leadership Department node) flagged for org hierarchy gap - Operations department now has no Department-level decision-maker.

questions.question: Which suppliers and ingredients go into Mango Chutney?
questions.answer: Mango Chutney depends on 4 ingredients from 3 suppliers. Mango Puree from TrueHarvest Co. is the most critical - it is single-sourced. The product is manufactured at the East Coast facility and distributed through retail and DTC channels.
reasoningSteps: Mango Chutney uses: Mango Puree, Cane Sugar, Citric Acid, Cayenne Pepper
Supplied by: TrueHarvest Co. (Mango Puree), Natural Flavors Co. (Citric Acid), Kroger Ingredients (Cane Sugar, Cayenne Pepper)
Manufactured at: East Coast Production Facility
Sold through: Retail Stores, E-Commerce DTC

questions.question: If we lose the Whole Foods account, which products are most affected?
questions.answer: Whole Foods carries 5 of our 10 products. Mango Chutney and Pesto Basilico have the highest concentration risk, with 40% and 35% of retail volume respectively flowing through this account. Garlic Aioli and Honey Mustard have better diversification across Target and regional chains.
reasoningSteps: Whole Foods carries: Mango Chutney, Garlic Aioli, Honey Mustard, Pesto Basilico, Apple Cider Vinegar, Sourdough Bread
Of these, Mango Chutney and Pesto Basilico have Whole Foods as their largest retail account
Garlic Aioli and Honey Mustard are also in Target and Chicago Outlet
Revenue concentration risk is highest for Mango Chutney (40% of retail volume through Whole Foods)
